title: Peanutty Filipino Rice Fried Rice
description: A yeasty and molten Filipino dish featuring peanut oil, this fried rice is a nostalgic treat, cooked to perfection in a scallop shell vessel and served raw-friendly with a side of khao khuah, a type of fry-grind-rice.
instructions: Heat the peanut oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet or wok, breaking up any clumps with a spatula.
Stir in the khao khuah and cook for about 2 minutes, until the rice is well coated.
Season the rice mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the rice mixture to the scalloped shell vessel.
Serve the Peanutty Filipino Rice Fried Rice immediately, garnished with chopped green onions if desired.
